data.narrative:
Castelion is a Series B defense and security company founded by Bryon Hargis, Sean Pitt, and Andrew Kreitz, currently valued at $1.0B+ post-money following $450M in total capital raised. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of 10 comparables—2 public and 8 private—operating in the same sector and stage, including Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Code Metal, and Defense Unicorns.

Two triangulation methods apply to this target. Stage-progression analysis uses the $1.0B last post-money valuation as a base and applies a typical Series B-to-subsequent-stage multiplier of 1.9x, implying $1.9B. Peer-median analysis across 7 same-sector, same-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $1.2B, with the peer set ranging from $300M to $1.5B. These two methods triangulate to a low of $1.1B, median of $1.6B, and high of $2.2B.

The median implied valuation of $1.6B sits materially above both the current $1.0B+ valuation and the peer median of $1.2B, reflecting the stage-progression method's upward bias. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the peer sample at the upper end of the defense tech market and the applicability of standard stage multipliers to this sector's longer sales cycles and customer concentration dynamics.
